exercises.title: Trace Objects.requireNonNull
exercises.scenario: requireNonNull returns value if non-null, throws if null.
hints: requireNonNull returns 'hello' when non-null
requireNonNull throws NullPointerException with the message when null

exercises.title: Add requireNonNull to constructor
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks to add null-safe validation.

blanks.hint: The Objects method that throws NPE if null

hints: Objects.requireNonNull(value, message)
Returns value if non-null; throws NullPointerException with message if null

exercises.title: Guard clauses — early returns
exercises.scenario: Rewrite nested if-else as flat guard clauses.
exercises.starter_code: // Rewrite discount() using guard clauses at the top
hints: Guard clauses: if (invalid condition) throw early
Two guards at the top: price <= 0 and loyaltyYears < 0
Main logic follows with no nesting

exercises.title: Defensive copy in constructor
exercises.scenario: Protect internal state from external mutation.
exercises.instructions:
Demonstrate that WITHOUT a defensive copy, the external list can corrupt internal state. Then FIX it with a defensive copy.

Expected output:
Without copy — corrupted: [A, B, HACKED]
With copy — protected: [A, B]
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// Demonstrate corruption then fix with defensive copy

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Without copy — corrupted: [A, B, HACKED]
With copy — protected: [A, B]

hints: BadConfig: this.settings = s — same object, caller can corrupt it
GoodConfig: this.settings = new ArrayList<>(s) — independent copy
Shows exactly why defensive copies matter

exercises.title: Full defensive constructor
exercises.scenario: All three defenses: null check, value validation, defensive copy.
exercises.instructions:
Write a defensive `Playlist` constructor:
- `Objects.requireNonNull` for name and songs
- `if name.isBlank()` → IllegalArgumentException
- Defensive copy of songs list
- Getter returns unmodifiable view

Expected output:
Playlist 'Rock Hits' with 2 songs
Cannot modify: yes
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// Defensive Playlist with null check + value validation + defensive copy

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Playlist 'Rock Hits' with 2 songs
Cannot modify: yes

hints: requireNonNull for name AND songs
isBlank() check after requireNonNull
new ArrayList<>(songs) in constructor, Collections.unmodifiableList in getter

exercises.title: Fix validation after usage
exercises.scenario: Null check must come BEFORE using the value.
exercises.instructions:
This code validates too late — the null check comes after a method call that will NPE. Fix it.

Expected output:
Processed: HELLO WORLD
Error: input must not be null

bugs.issue: Null check comes AFTER input.toUpperCase() — will NPE before reaching the check
bugs.fix: Move null check to BEFORE using input

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Processed: HELLO WORLD
Error: input must not be null

hints: The null check must come BEFORE any use of input
Move the if-null check to be the first line
The toUpperCase call must only happen after confirming input is non-null

exercises.title: Fail-fast method validation
exercises.scenario: Validate all inputs before any computation.
exercises.instructions:
Write `createLoan(String borrower, double principal, double annualRate, int months)` that:
- requireNonNull borrower, not blank
- principal > 0
- annualRate 0-100
- months 1-360

Fails immediately on first bad input with specific message.

Expected output:
Loan: Aisha ₹100000 @ 8.5% for 24m
Error: annualRate must be 0-100, got: 150.0
Error: borrower must not be blank
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.Objects;

// createLoan with full fail-fast validation

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Loan: Aisha ₹100000.0 @ 8.5% for 24m
Error: annualRate must be 0-100, got: 150.0
Error: borrower must not be blank

hints: requireNonNull first, then isBlank, then numeric range checks
Each check with specific message including the invalid value
record creates a compact immutable data class

exercises.title: Immutable class design
exercises.scenario: Build a fully immutable value class.
exercises.instructions:
Design an immutable `Money` class:
- `final` fields: amount (double), currency (String)
- `final` class
- No setters
- Arithmetic returns NEW Money objects
- Defensive: amount must be >= 0, currency not blank

Expected output:
₹500.00 INR
₹1500.00 INR
₹300.00 INR
hints: final class prevents subclassing
final fields prevent reassignment
add/subtract return NEW Money objects — original unchanged

exercises.title: Optional for nullable find
exercises.scenario: Return Optional instead of null for 'might not exist' results.
exercises.instructions:
Implement `findEmployee(Map<String, String> employees, String id)` returning `Optional<String>`. Demonstrate isPresent(), orElse(), map().

Expected output:
Found: Aisha Khan
Not found: No employee
Email: aisha.khan@company.com

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Found: Aisha Khan
Not found: No employee
Email: aisha.khan@company.com

hints: Optional.ofNullable(value) wraps nullable — returns Optional.empty() for null
orElse(default) returns value or default
map(transform) applies function if present

exercises.title: Trace defensive copy protection
exercises.scenario: Understand what happens with and without defensive copy.
exercises.expected_solution:
Team: [Alice, Bob]
List: [Alice, Bob, Hacker]
Blocked!

test_cases.expected:
Team: [Alice, Bob]
List: [Alice, Bob, Hacker]
Blocked!

hints: Defensive copy: team.members is an independent copy
Adding to original list doesn't affect team
getMembers() returns unmodifiable — add throws

exercises.title: Validation boundary — public vs private
exercises.scenario: Validate at the public boundary, trust in private.
exercises.instructions:
Design `ReportService` with:
- `public generateReport(String title, List<String> sections)` — validates everything
- `private formatSection(String section)` — trusts input (private, called only from validated path)

Show public validates, private just processes.

Expected output:
=== Sales Q1 ===
• REVENUE
• EXPENSES
Error: title required
Error: sections cannot be empty
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// Public validates, private trusts

test_cases.expected_stdout:
=== Sales Q1 ===
• REVENUE
• EXPENSES
Error: title required
Error: sections cannot be empty

hints: Public method: Objects.requireNonNull + isBlank + isEmpty checks
Private method: no validation — trusts it's called with valid data
This is the validation boundary principle

exercises.title: UserProfile — full defensive class
exercises.scenario: Combine all defensive patterns in one class.
exercises.instructions:
Build a defensively programmed `UserProfile`:
- requireNonNull + isBlank on name, email
- email must contain '@'
- age must be 13-120
- List<String> interests — defensive copy in/out
- All fields final except interests (can addInterest)
- addInterest validates non-null, non-blank

Expected output:
Profile: Aisha (28) aisha@test.com
Interests: [coding, reading]
Error: email must contain @
Error: age must be 13-120, got: 200
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// Full defensive UserProfile class

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Profile: Aisha (28) aisha@test.com
Interests: [coding, reading]
Error: email must contain @
Error: age must be 13-120, got: 200

hints: Defensive copy of interests list in constructor
getInterests() returns unmodifiable view
Adding 'HACKED' to original list doesn't affect profile — defensive copy works

exercises.title: ProductCatalog with full defensive design
exercises.scenario: All defensive patterns in a multi-method class.
exercises.instructions:
Build `ProductCatalog` with full defensive design:
- `addProduct(String sku, String name, double price)` — validates all, no duplicates
- `updatePrice(String sku, double newPrice)` — validates sku exists, price > 0
- `getCatalog()` — returns unmodifiable Map
- `productsUnder(double maxPrice)` — returns defensive List (not internal refs)

Expected output:
Added: MOUSE-01 Mouse ₹599.0
Added: KB-01 Keyboard ₹1999.0
Updated MOUSE-01 → ₹549.0
Error: SKU already exists: MOUSE-01
Error: SKU not found: GHOST-99
Under ₹1000: [MOUSE-01: ₹549.0]

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Added: MOUSE-01 Mouse ₹599.0
Added: KB-01 Keyboard ₹1999.0
Updated MOUSE-01 → ₹549.0
Error: SKU already exists: MOUSE-01
Error: SKU not found: GHOST-99
Under ₹1000: [MOUSE-01: ₹549.0]

hints: addProduct: null checks, blank checks, price > 0, duplicate SKU check
updatePrice: null check, existence check, price > 0
productsUnder returns new unmodifiable list — not internal refs

exercises.title: Transaction validator — fail-fast pipeline
exercises.scenario: Validate before processing — fail at first invalid step.
exercises.instructions:
Build `TransactionValidator.validate(Transaction t)` — validates in order:
1. null check on transaction
2. fromAccountId not blank
3. toAccountId not blank, not same as from
4. amount 1.0 to 1,000,000
5. description not over 200 chars

Return a `ValidationResult(boolean valid, String error)`. Use fail-fast: return first error found.

Expected output:
valid=true
valid=false error=fromAccountId required
valid=false error=cannot transfer to same account
valid=false error=amount must be 1.0-1000000.0, got: 0.5

test_cases.expected_stdout:
valid=true
valid=false error=fromAccountId required
valid=false error=cannot transfer to same account
valid=false error=amount must be 1.0-1000000.0, got: 0.5

hints: Return ValidationResult.fail(message) on first invalid — don't continue
Fail-fast: each check returns immediately on failure
record is a compact Java 16+ data class

exercises.title: Complete defensive service — all patterns
exercises.scenario: Full defensive class combining every pattern from this topic.
exercises.instructions:
Build `AuditedPaymentService` with EVERY defensive pattern:
1. Constructor: requireNonNull + isBlank for serviceName, defensive copy of allowedCurrencies Set
2. `transfer(from, to, amount, currency)`: null checks, blank checks, amount > 0, currency in allowedSet, from != to
3. `getTransactionHistory()`: returns unmodifiable List
4. `getAllowedCurrencies()`: returns unmodifiable Set
5. Optional return: `findLastTransfer(String accountId)` returns Optional<String>

Expected output:
Transfer complete: A001→A002 ₹5000.0 INR
Error: currency not supported: USD
Error: cannot transfer to same account
Allowed: [INR, SGD]
Last for A001: A001→A002 ₹5000.0 INR
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// AuditedPaymentService — all defensive patterns

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Transfer complete: A001→A002 ₹5000.0 INR
Error: currency not supported: USD
Error: cannot transfer to same account
Allowed: [INR, SGD]
Last for A001: A001→A002 ₹5000.0 INR

hints: Defensive copy of Set: new HashSet<>(currencies) — adding USD to original doesn't affect service
currencies is [INR, SGD] in constructor — USD is added AFTER, so not in service's copy
Optional.of() when found, Optional.empty() when not
getAllowedCurrencies() returns unmodifiable set — sort a copy for display
